summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting that included swearing in Trustees Lisa Allen and Dan Hodges for new terms, swearing in four new firefighters, and presenting life-saving awards to three firefighters who resuscitated a cardiac arrest patient. A zoning case to rezone a property on State Route 28 from B2 to M2 heavy industrial was denied unanimously due to spot zoning concerns and incompatibility with the Town Center Overlay District, though the board expressed willingness to work with the applicant on alternatives. The board also approved a zoning text amendment increasing minimum lot sizes, approved purchase of a John Deere wheel loader for $115,000, and entered a collaborative resurfacing agreement with Clermont County.
decisions: Motion to amend the agenda to include item N1 (resurfacing agreement resolution) passed unanimously
Motion to adopt the agenda as amended passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills (Nov 18 - Dec 9, 2025) passed unanimously
Resolution denying map amendment from B2 to M2 heavy industrial for 1800 State Route 28 (PIDN 112-207-J207) passed unanimously (zoning case ZC-2025-009 denied)
Resolution R031-2025 to initiate text amendments to Section 6.02C3 increasing PUDR minimum lot size to 10,800 sq ft passed unanimously
Resolution R030-2025 amending 2025 budget for EMS billing revenue and Station 18 building fund passed unanimously
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Chief Myers to FDIC in Indianapolis (April 21-25, 2026) passed unanimously
Motion to increase 2025 appropriations in gasoline tax motor vehicles fund by $115,000 passed (Allen yes, Hauserman yes, Hodges abstain)
Motion to approve purchase of 2024 John Deere 244P wheel loader from Murphy Tractor for $115,000 passed (Allen yes, Hauserman yes, Hodges abstain)
Motion to sell 2017 Case 580 Super M backhoe on GovDeals with $40,000 reserve passed (Allen yes, Hauserman yes, Hodges abstain)
Motion to accept $790 in food gift cards from anonymous donor for police, fire, and service passed unanimously
Motion to cancel January 13, 2026 meeting passed unanimously
Motion to set January 27, 2026 as organizational and regular meeting passed unanimously
Resolution R033-2025 to request America 250-Ohio flag and promotional materials passed unanimously
Resolution R034-2025 approving resurfacing agreement with Clermont County for township road improvements passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 7:22 PM passed unanimously
action_items: Zoning Director Langenheim to work with Haywood family applicant to explore B2-compatible alternatives including PDB/PUD options or use variance for their property at 1800 State Route 28
Zoning Director Langenheim to continue work on future land use plan changes with target completion around March 2026
Director Meadows to list 2017 Case 580 Super M backhoe on GovDeals with $40,000 reserve
Administrator Rose to request two America 250-Ohio flags (one for Historical Society, one for township display)
Chief Myers to attend FDIC conference in Indianapolis April 21-25, 2026
Zoning Director Langenheim to prepare and distribute community surveys related to future land use plan
Haywood family encouraged to develop building plans and explore alternative zoning approaches and return with detailed proposal
key_amounts: $115,000 for purchase of 2024 John Deere 244P wheel loader from Murphy Tractor
$140,000 retail value of the wheel loader being purchased for $115,000
$40,000 reserve minimum for sale of 2017 Case 580 Super M backhoe on GovDeals
$790 in food gift cards accepted from anonymous donor for police, fire, and service staff
$600,000 estimated paving costs contingent on OPWC grant approval
10,800 square feet proposed new minimum lot size for PUDR (up from 5,000 sq ft)
60-month warranty included with wheel loader purchase
notable_topics: Swearing in of Trustees Lisa Allen and Dan Hodges for new terms
Swearing in of four new firefighters: Anthony Addison, Jameson Benoit, Mueller, and Tyler Pilcher
Life-saving awards presented to firefighters Anthony Addison, Nathan Denowden, and Tyler Harding for cardiac arrest save on September 26, 2025
Patient Ms. Brock publicly thanked the firefighters who saved her life
Zoning case ZC-2025-009 denial of B2 to M2 rezoning for Haywood Utility Contractors property on State Route 28 - spot zoning and Town Center Overlay District conflict concerns
Station 18 construction update - on pace for April grand opening, interior stairwells and exhaust system complete, sprinkler system passed final hydro test
Text amendment to increase PUDR minimum lot size from 5,000 to 10,800 square feet with 30-foot setback requirements
America 250 Goshen mural dedication recognition and America 250-Ohio flag request
Collaborative resurfacing agreement with Clermont County for township road improvements contingent on OPWC grant
Future land use plan changes in progress with community surveys planned, target completion March 2026 to align with moratorium timeline
Budget amendment for EMS billing revenue shortfall due to state Medicaid managed care payment delays
First snowstorm of season handled without problems by service department
Trustee Hodges abstained from wheel loader and backhoe votes due to employment with Murphy Tractor
attendees: Fiscal Officer Laura (last name not stated)
Stan Beck (Beck Hardware owner, public speaker)
Jean Schmidbauer (Goshen Historical Society Secretary, mentioned in letter)
notes: Trustee Hodges disclosed employment with Murphy Tractor and Equipment and abstained from votes on items I, J, and K related to the wheel loader purchase and backhoe sale. The Clermont County Planning Commission recommended approval of the B2 to M2 rezoning with conditions, but the Goshen Township Zoning Commission unanimously recommended denial, and the Board of Trustees also unanimously denied it. The board encouraged the applicant to work with zoning staff on alternative approaches such as a Planned Development Business (PDB) or use variance to achieve their goals without M2 zoning. EMS billing revenue shortfall attributed to Ohio Director of Medicaid delaying managed care plan payments until mid-2026. The January 13, 2026 meeting was canceled and reorganizational meeting set for January 27, 2026. Station 18 construction costs rolling into 2026 due to county permitting delays. The board noted this was the first meeting all year without an executive session.
title: BOT 12-9-25
